rationale: All three lenses ran and produced usable results. The production guard is correctly placed at BaseAgentAdapter.ts:755 — after the else-if(!isWindows) block and before let finalCommand — and its regex matches the existing quoting branch at line 713. Three unit tests cover the three behavioral branches (null-path+metachar → quoted, null-path+clean → unchanged, resolved-path+metachar → not double-quoted), confirmed by 154/154 test files passing. Standards: commit fix(cli): ... passes commitlint type-enum (fix) and scope-enum (cli). No blocking findings. Five deferred observations noted in rationale; four dismissed as non-applicable.

business_review.criterion: Special characters in Windows username path no longer crash CLI
business_review.notes: isWindows + /[ ()&|<>^%[\]{}]/.test + !startsWith('"') guard wraps commandPath before shell:true spawn.

business_review.criterion: CLI properly escapes all valid Windows directory names
business_review.notes: Regex covers the full CMD.EXE metacharacter set; identical pattern used at the existing quoting branch (line 713).
Cannot verify from a diff; no execution evidence for a real profile path containing parentheses.

business_review.criterion: Regression tests ensure directory name edge cases (spaces, parentheses) do not break CLI
business_review.notes: Three tests cover the three behavioral branches (parentheses path, clean path, non-null resolved path). Space-path test not present; guard handles spaces identically to parentheses (same regex branch). Deferred: adding a fourth test case.

business_review.criterion: Fix placed after else-if(!isWindows) block, before let finalCommand
business_review.notes: Confirmed from source read: guard is at line 755, let finalCommand at line 761.

business_review.criterion: Regex /[ ()&|<>^%[\]{}]/ matches existing quoting branch at line 713
business_review.notes: Identical character class at both locations.

business_review.criterion: Guard !commandPath.startsWith('"') prevents double-quoting when path already quoted
business_review.notes: Test 3 exercises this: if the check were removed, commandPath would be double-quoted and Test 3 would fail.

business_review.criterion: Three specified unit tests all pass
business_review.notes: 22 tests in BaseAgentAdapter.test.ts pass; 2259/2259 tests pass overall.

standards_review.notes: fix(cli): quote Windows commandPath when getCommandPath returns null — type 'fix' and scope 'cli' both in type-enum and scope-enum; subject 57 chars, under 100-char limit. Commit passed commitlint hook.
Typecheck step in pre-commit hook exited 0.
lint-staged ran during pre-commit without warnings or errors.
No new user-controlled input surface introduced. commandPath originates from plugin metadata, not from untrusted external input.

standards_review.standard: No child_process.exec directly (use exec() from utils/processes.ts)
standards_review.notes: Change does not introduce any new exec() calls; it only modifies the commandPath string before the existing spawn() call.

deferred: EC-001: Malformed pre-quoted path ('"path' without closing quote) bypasses !startsWith guard — theoretical, plugin metadata does not produce half-open quotes.
EC-002: No test for space in path — guard handles spaces identically to parentheses (same regex branch); three behavioral branches fully covered.
EC-003: Missing test for already-quoted-null-path case — edge case, plugin metadata does not pre-quote commandPath.
EC-004: No test for guard + non-empty args interaction — minor, args concatenation has independent coverage.
EC-005: Unescaped [ in regex character class — pre-existing at line 713; not introduced by this PR.
dismissed: Blind: Embedded '"' in commandPath via injection — Windows forbids '"' in filesystem paths; not reachable.
Blind: Mock setup concern — all 22 tests in file pass; mocks are correctly configured.
Blind: Real getCommandPath called — mocks are in place; tests pass.
AC-001/AC-002: Truncated-diff artifacts from acceptance lens — full diff confirmed as correct from source read.
